# Transcrição: Use_this_technique_to_crush_your_next_job_interview

- **Fonte:** Use_this_technique_to_crush_your_next_job_interview
- **Duração:** 01:33
- **Idioma:** en
- **Modelo:** small

---

## [00:00 - 01:33]

The biggest advice I have for people who go into interviews is storytelling. Imagine you were talking to me and then basically I'm in the interview and you ask me a question, you say, uh, haven't can you tell me a bit about yourself? Sure, I'm reliable. I'm punctual. I'm hard working. And I believe in your company values. In me saying all those words, you've learned nothing about me. Whereas ultimately there comes a time in an interview where you'll hear the interviewer say, well, hey, Vin, why don't you tell me a little bit about yourself? Oh, thank you, Jasmine. Jasmine, when I was young, I remember I almost finished my university degree and I was six months away from finishing my university degree. I came home and I said to my parents, Mum, dad, I want to quit university and, you know, being Asian parents, they'll think to themselves, you're not going to fulfill the Asian prophecy. What the hell? And it was a very difficult time for me. But, but the thing is I wanted to pursue something that I loved, you know, and something that I love Jasmine is having a career here with all of you. I didn't want to become an accountant. I wanted to become a designer and that's why I'm here today. You know, and it's again, even though it's against Mum and Dad's wishes, it's something that I want to do. Now again, contrast that with I'm punctual, I'm trustworthy and you're just listing a bunch of things. Whereas the powerful thing about storytelling, the other person builds rapport with you and they go, wow, this person seems amazing. I like this person. It's because you're storytelling. And in essence, once you've got connection to rapport, you are way more influential as the interviewer and you're more likely to get the position.
